nodes.x: Help, Lord; for the godly man ceaseth; for the faithful fail from among the children of men.
Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.

suitable way in his word, and made ample provision whereby the faithful servants whom he sends shall lack for nothing, the children of men have run to two extremes opposite to each other, and both erroneous. To correct these extremes, and call attention to the Lord’s own way, is our object at present.

And again, ‘from the declarations of the Savior to his servant John in his apocalyptic vision, vc learn that to him that overcometh »mi keepeth my works unto the end, to him will [ give power over the notions, and he shall rule with a rod of iron; as the vessels of the potter shall they be broken to shivers; even *s [ received of my Father, and I will give unto him the morniug star.’ Again, to…

Therefore now, Lord God of Israel, keep with thy servant David my father that thou promisedst him, saying, There shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit on the throne of Israel; so that thy children take heed to their way, that they walk before me as thou hast walked before me.
They speak vanity every one with his neighbour: with flattering lips and with a double heart do they speak.
For when they speak great swelling words of vanity, they allure through the lusts of the flesh, through much wantonness, those that were clean escaped from them who live in error.
Surely God will not hear vanity, neither will the Almighty regard it.
Their tongue is as an arrow shot out; it speaketh deceit: one speaketh peaceably to his neighbour with his mouth, but in heart he layeth his wait.
For with stammering lips and another tongue will he speak to this people.
Whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
The Lord shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things:
Lying lips are abomination to the Lord: but they that deal truly are his delight.
Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?
Take with you words, and turn to the Lord: say unto him, Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously: so will we render the calves of our lips.
My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.
Who have said, With our tongue will we prevail; our lips are our own: who is lord over us?
The Lord shall cut off all flattering lips, and the tongue that speaketh proud things:
The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: but he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction.
For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, now will I arise, saith the Lord; I will set him in safety from him that puffeth at him.
And the firstborn of the poor shall feed, and the needy shall lie down in safety; and I will kill thy root with famine, and he shall slay thy remnant.

But thus saith the Lord, even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered; for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.
And the firstborn of the poor shall feed, and the needy shall lie down in safety: and I will kill thy root with famine, and he shall slay thy remnant.
He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.

But thus saith the Lord, Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible shall be delivered: for I will contend with him that contendeth with thee, and I will save thy children.
The words of the Lord are pure words: as silver tried in a furnace of earth, purified seven times.
As silver is melted in the midst of the furnace, so shall ye be melted in the midst thereof; and ye shall know that I the Lord have poured out my fury upon you.
Behold, I have refined thee, but not with silver; I have chosen thee in the furnace of affliction.

6. And a command has gone forth from the presence of the Lord concerning
those who dwell on the earth that their ruin is accomplished because
they have learnt all the secrets of the angels, and all the violence of
the Satans, and all their powers—the most secret ones—and all the power
of those who practise sorcery, and the power of witchcraft, and the
power of those who make molten images for the…
Of the gold, the silver, and the brass, and the iron, there is no number. Arise therefore, and be doing, and the Lord be with thee.
Thou shalt keep them, O Lord, thou shalt preserve them from this generation for ever.
But thou, O Lord, shalt endure for ever; and thy remembrance unto all generations.

Thou shalt keep therefore his statutes, and his commandments, which I command thee this day, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy days upon the earth, which the Lord thy God giveth thee, for ever.
And inasmuch as they are faithful they shall be preserved, and I, the Lord, will be with them.
Thou, O Lord, remainest for ever; thy throne from generation to generation.
Thou shalt therefore keep the commandments, and the statutes, and the judgments, which I command thee this day, to do them.
The wicked walk on every side, when the vilest men are exalted.
Which goeth in company with the workers of iniquity, and walketh with wicked men.
Hast thou marked the old way which wicked men have trodden?
There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
